steps.title: Identify the noun the participial phrase must modify
steps.content: The participial phrase “Established in 1936 by African American novelist Richard Wright” describes something specific that Richard Wright founded. The subject immediately following the comma must be that thing.

steps.title: Verify the logical connection
steps.content:
Choice A puts “the South Side Writers Group” as the subject after the comma—Richard Wright established this group in 1936, so the participial phrase correctly modifies it.

Choices B, C, and D have subjects (writers, Chicago, Chicago writers) that don't align with what Richard Wright actually founded.

The semicolon correctly joins two independent clauses describing the group: what it provided and what it would become.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
